rulings.Case Title: BHAGBAT KHANAL ET AL VS. RI LONG LIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel (1) Production Of Plaintiff Bhagbat Khanals Cell Phone(S) For Forensic Inspection; And (2) Further Responses To Defendants Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents And Things, Set Six; And
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY CALENDAR, for Tuesday, May-27-2025, LINE 5. DEFENDANTS RI LIN, ENTERPRISE RENT-A-CAR COMPANY OF SAN FRANCISCO, EAN HOLDINGS, LLC, and ENTERPRISE RENT-A-CAR OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Compel (1) Production Of Plaintiff Bhagbat Khanals Cell Phone(S) For Forensic Inspection; And (2) Further Responses To Defendants Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents And Things, Set Six. Defendants' motion to compel production of cell phones, further responses to production requests 38-40 and for monetary sanctions is granted as to the production of the cell phones and further responses subject to an order providing adequate safeguards for protection of plaintiff's privacy interests in information other than plaintiff's calls with his wife and food delivery and rideshare activities and denied as to monetary sanctions. Plaintiff's request for monetary sanctions is also denied. Defendants have shown that the discovery they seek is directly relevant to the disputed issues in this case and that plaintiff's' objections based on relevance lack merit. Plaintiff has waived his privacy rights relating to information pertaining to calls with his wife and his food delivery and rideshare activities but not to any other information that may be on his cell phones. The parties are required to meet and confer regarding the protocols for the forensic inspection to ensure that there are adequate safeguards for protection of plaintiff's privacy interests in information other than plaintiff's calls with his wife and food delivery and rideshare activities. Both sides acted with substantial justification, so both sides' requests for monetary sanctions are denied. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS HENRY VS. JAMES M. NADEAU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses Re Defendants James M. Nadeau And Lisa A Nadeaus Responses To Form Interrogatories Set One And Request For Production Of Documents Set One And Seek Sanctionss
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY CALENDAR, for Tuesday, May-27-2025, LINE 6. PLAINTIFF THOMAS HENRY'S Motion To Compel Further Responses Re Defendants James M. Nadeau And Lisa A Nadeaus Responses To Form Interrogatories Set One And Request For Production Of Documents Set One And Seek Sanctions. Plaintiff Thomas Henry's motion to compel the Nadeaus to provide further responses to set one form interrogatories and set one document requests and for monetary sanctions is granted in part and denied in part as to the motion to compel and denied as to monetary sanctions. The Nadeaus' request for monetary sanctions is also denied. Form interrogatory 4.1. Due to the uncertainty whether the response is complete, the Nadeaus must provide a further response stating that they have provided all requested information as to all policies, including any excess policies. Form interrogatory 12.7 and Requests 1, 4, 6, 15, . Due to the Nadeaus' making their responses subject to objections and their failure to explain how they understand each of the terms they contend are vague, the Nadeaus must provide further responses that are not subject to any objections and explain their understanding of the terms they contend are vague. Requests 2, 9, 10, 13, 14, 16, 18, 19, 20, 21, 22, and 26. The requests are overbroad and possibly cover a large amount of information that does not meet the standard of discovery relevance and thus no further responses are required. Requests 5, 11, 12, 17,and 23. The Nadeaus must provide further responses stating whether they do or do not have any responsive documents in their possession, custody or control and explaining their understanding of each of the terms they contend are vague. Requests 7, 8 and 27. The Nadeaus have agreed to provide further responses and must do so. Both sides acted with substantial justification, so both sides' requests for monetary sanctions are denied. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)
